originalTitle: This 1 Momentum ETF Is Still Beating the S&P 500 With Lower Drawdowns
summary: Most ETFs that chase outperformance get crushed when the market turns, yet one momentum fund keeps defying that logic cycle after cycle. The explanation behind how it sidesteps the worst downturns while still beating the index is more straightforward than you might expect.
content:
It shouldn’t be possible to outperform the S&P 500 time and time again without getting hit by downturns as hard. However, the Invesco S&P 500 Momentum ETF  ( NYSEARCA:SPMO ) should make you think otherwise. Obviously, SPMO is unlikely to continue outperforming the SPY forever or become a default for investors, but it is still worth looking into given  how solid it has been.

SPMO has delivered 537% in gains in the past decade, whereas the SPY has delivered 306%. Over the five-year period , the SPMO has delivered nearly double the S&P 500’s gains. And each downturn over the past five years saw the SPMO stay neck-and-neck with the S&P 500, or even outperform it at times.

Only in the past month has the SPY outperformed the SPMO, but this may not last, as the SPY is declining to close the gap.

SPMO essentially just holds the winners. The theory is that winners keep winning, so it uses the S&P 500 Momentum index to pick the 100 highest-scoring stocks in the S&P 500. It rebalances twice a year, and the formula has been working well during this AI rally.

All of its stocks are in the S&P 500, so the ETF remains invested in solid businesses without leaning too aggressively on momentum.

You pay a 0.13% expense ratio, or $13 per $10,000.

It’s more expensive compared to the VOO or the SPY, but the fee is still marginal.

How SPMO keeps outperforming even during downturns

It has always been a winner’s market, but the gap between winners and losers has only widened in the past few years. Tech stocks on a winning streak have a remarkable tendency to keep winning. This has allowed SPMO to keep beating the S&P 500 and even the Nasdaq-100 year after year. It simply holds a concentrated basket of outperformers the market keeps on rewarding.

What’s surprising is that these holdings haven’t declined as much during market downturns. I’d attribute this to SPMO holding the top 100 highest-performing stocks in the S&P 500. Yes, it does have more exposure to large tech stocks, but picking so many S&P 500 stocks also adds some safety, specifically from businesses with healthy financials.

The rebalancing is also quick enough that each time a stock turns into a loser or stalls, the ETF can move out and rotate into a winner in time. It has been a recurring theme: soaring tech stocks like Nvidia ( NASDAQ :NVDA | NVDA Price Prediction ) and Palantir ( NASDAQ :PLTR ) stall for a few months before undergoing a sharp correction. This gives SPMO time to exit stocks that are losing their luster, while the S&P 500 holds on to them.

SPMO is unlikely to perpetually outperform the S&P 500 . That said, if you are confident the broader AI rally will continue, it’s a good idea to buy and hold this as a satellite investment. As long as the broader market keeps rallying, SPMO should continue to deliver.

The biggest risk you have here is if the stock market sells off in tandem and does so very sharply. Since SPMO holds stocks with the most momentum, a sharp reversal like that will cause SPMO to decline much more if the crash is severe enough. Worse, if the crash happens just after a rebalancing, it will take SPMO 6 more months to exit these losing names. But again, you need a very unlucky series of events to line up perfectly to hurt SPMO.

Admittedly, SPMO also remains untested against a true recession. If you are going to buy, remember that you cannot buy and hold this through multiple market cycles. Holding the top 100 “momentum” stocks in a market that does not have any momentum to offer has not been tested previously.

Regardless, if you think the bears are wrong and the bull market has a year or two to rally before anything breaks, consider buying.

Contact [email protected] for any questions or corrections.
chineseTitle: 该 1 动量 ETF 仍以较低回撤击败标准普尔 500 指数
chineseSummary: 大多数追逐优异表现的 ETF 在市场转向时都会被击垮，但一只动量基金却不断地在一个又一个周期中违背这一逻辑。它如何避开最严重的经济衰退同时仍然跑赢指数背后的解释比你想象的更简单。
chineseContent:
如果不遭受经济低迷的严重打击，就不可能一次又一次跑赢标准普尔 500 指数。然而，Invesco S&P 500 Momentum ETF (NYSEARCA:SPMO) 应该会让您有不同的想法。显然，SPMO 不太可能永远跑赢 SPY 或成为投资者的默认选择，但鉴于它的稳健程度，它仍然值得研究。

SPMO 在过去十年中实现了 537% 的收益，而 SPY 实现了 306%。五年期间，SPMO 的涨幅几乎是标准普尔 500 指数的两倍。过去五年的每一次经济衰退中，SPMO 的表现都与标准普尔 500 指数并驾齐驱，有时甚至跑赢了标准普尔 500 指数。

仅在过去一个月，SPY 的表现才优于 SPMO，但这可能不会持续下去，因为 SPY 正在缩小差距。

SPMO 本质上只保留获胜者。其理论是赢家不断获胜，因此它使用标准普尔 500 动量指数来挑选标准普尔 500 指数中得分最高的 100 只股票。它每年重新平衡两次，并且该公式在本次人工智能反弹期间一直运行良好。

其所有股票均属于标准普尔 500 指数成分股，因此该 ETF 仍投资于稳健的企业，而不会过度依赖势头。

您支付 0.13% 的费用率，即每 10,000 美元支付 13 美元。

与 VOO 或 SPY 相比，它的价格更高，但费用仍然很低。

SPMO 如何在经济低迷时期保持优异表现

它一直是赢家的市场，但赢家和输家之间的差距在过去几年里只增不减。连续上涨的科技股有显着的持续上涨趋势。这使得 SPMO 能够年复一年地击败标准普尔 500 指数，甚至击败纳斯达克 100 指数。它只是集中了一篮子表现优异的股票，市场不断给予回报。

令人惊讶的是，在市场低迷时期，这些持有量并没有下降那么多。我将此归因于 SPMO 持有标准普尔 500 指数中表现最好的 100 只股票。是的，它确实对大型科技股有更多的投资，但选择如此多的标准普尔 500 指数股票也增加了一些安全性，特别是来自财务状况良好的企业。

重新平衡的速度也足够快，每次一只股票变成输家或停滞不前时，ETF 都可以及时退出并转向赢家。这是一个反复出现的主题：Nvidia（纳斯达克股票代码：NVDA | NVDA 价格预测）和 Palantir（纳斯达克股票代码：PLTR）等飙升的科技股在经历大幅调整之前停滞了几个月。这使得 SPMO 有时间退出正在失去光彩的股票，而标准普尔 500 指数则持有这些股票。

SPMO 不太可能永远跑赢标准普尔 500 指数。也就是说，如果你有信心更广泛的人工智能反弹将持续下去，那么购买并持有它作为卫星投资是个好主意。只要大盘持续上涨，SPMO 就应该继续发挥作用。

这里面临的最大风险是股市是否同步抛售，而且抛售幅度非常大。由于 SPMO 持有势头最强的股票，如果崩盘足够严重，这样的急剧反转将导致 SPMO 跌幅更大。更糟糕的是，如果崩盘发生在重新平衡之后，SPMO 还需要 6 个月的时间才能退出这些亏损的公司。但同样，你需要一系列非常不幸的事件才能完美地排列起来才能伤害 SPMO。

诚然，SPMO 还没有经过针对真正衰退的考验。如果您要购买，请记住您无法在多个市场周期中购买并持有它。在没有任何动量提供的市场中持有前 100 只“动量”股票以前从未经过过测试。

无论如何，如果您认为空头是错误的，并且牛市在任何突破之前还有一两年的反弹时间，请考虑买入。

如有任何问题或更正，请联系 [email protected]。
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于财经市场信号，适合用于观察利率、汇率、股债商品、金融机构和宏观政策变化。
